calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_average_r.title: Average rates of change
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_average_r.reason: Average rate of change is pre-derivative setup; no calculus Limits chapter required.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_definitio.title: Definition of the derivative
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_definitio.reason: The limit definition of the derivative is the skill that actually requires Limits (and average rates as the difference quotient).

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_instantan.title: Instantaneous rates of change
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_instantan.reason: Instantaneous rate is the derivative at a point; builds on the definition, not a fresh Limits chapter tour.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_power_rul.title: Power Rule
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_power_rul.reason: Power Rule is a differentiation method after the derivative concept is introduced; does not re-require Limits overview.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_higher_or.title: Higher order derivatives
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_higher_or.reason: Higher-order derivatives repeatedly apply basic differentiation rules (starting with Power Rule).

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_product_r.title: Product Rule
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_product_r.reason: Product Rule extends basic differentiation; Power Rule fluency is assumed.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_quotient_.title: Quotient Rule
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_quotient_.reason: Quotient Rule builds on Power Rule and is closely related to Product Rule.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_chain_rul.title: Chain Rule
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_chain_rul.reason: Chain Rule composes basic differentiation methods (power/product/quotient).

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_rules_usi.title: Rules, using tables
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_rules_usi.reason: Table-based rule problems mix Power, Product, Quotient, and Chain.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_trigonome.title: Trigonometric
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_trigonome.reason: As generated: Easy is basic sin/cos/tan; Medium uses sin(kx) (chain with constant); Hard mixes product and chain. Not a Limits dependency.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_inverse_t.title: Inverse trigonometric
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_inverse_t.reason: Inverse trig derivatives use trig derivative forms and the Chain Rule.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_natural_l.title: Natural logarithms and exponentials
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_natural_l.reason: Natural exp/log derivatives use basic rules and the Chain Rule.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_other_bas.title: Other base logarithms and exponentials
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_other_bas.reason: Other-base exp/log reduce to natural exp/log derivatives.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_logarithm.title: Logarithmic
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_logarithm.reason: Logarithmic differentiation combines log derivatives with Product/Chain.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_implicit.title: Implicit
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_implicit.reason: Implicit differentiation applies Product and Chain Rules to implicitly defined relations.

calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_inverse_f.title: Inverse functions
calculus:derivatives:calc_diff_inverse_f.reason: Inverse-function derivatives rely on the Chain Rule relationship.
